content.markdown:
Wave function collapse is an algorithm generating tile based images based off simple configuration or sample images.

I developed a simple tool that lets you load in tiles, edit edges, configure certain parameters and generate images.

I developed a simple tool that lets you load in tiles, edit edges, configure certain parameters and generate images.

I developed a simple tool that lets you load in tiles, edit edges, configure certain parameters and generate images.

content.markdown:
Designed and produced Menus for [Brickhaus Taps & Brews](https://www.instagram.com/brickhaus_tapsnbrews/), a Cafe + Bar in Nagpur, India. 

The menu was designed in a custom tool for creating offset booklets as an ongoing research project on alternative interfaces for publication design.

The menu was designed in a custom tool for creating offset booklets as an ongoing research project on alternative interfaces for publication design.

content.markdown:
[Aryaman](https://github.com/aryamancodes/) Made a search engine for XKCD Comics in GO. He asked me nicely to make a frontend for it. The frontend exposes all of the traffic passing through the client and responses from the server.

[LINK](https://xkcd-search.netlify.app/)

content.markdown:
A visual archive of ways in which one documents their work categorized into three taxonomies. I constructed a modular publication where succeeding pages complete the current page—creating multiple layers of interaction.

Created for Ali Qadeer's Research Methods class.
content.html:
A visual archive of ways in which one documents their work categorized into three taxonomies. I constructed a modular publication where succeeding pages complete the current page—creating multiple layers of interaction.

Created for Ali Qadeer's Research Methods class.

A visual archive of ways in which one documents their work categorized into three taxonomies. I constructed a modular publication where succeeding pages complete the current page—creating multiple layers of interaction.
Created for Ali Qadeer's Research Methods class.

content.markdown:
This project was designed in a custom web based tool. For a sneak peak of how this tool looks and behaves, [check this recording.](https://are.na/block/24747610). The arcs make shapes based on the proximity of text boxes to one another. Lines exist.

The tool has ephemeral qualities of liveness and performance inscribed into it, leaving the marks of text boxes as they move. Marks slowly disappear over time. This project is an attempt to re frame graphic design as a performative practice.
content.html:
This project was designed in a custom web based tool. For a sneak peak of how this tool looks and behaves, check this recording.. The arcs make shapes based on the proximity of text boxes to one another. Lines exist.

The tool has ephemeral qualities of liveness and performance inscribed into it, leaving the marks of text boxes as they move. Marks slowly disappear over time. This project is an attempt to re frame graphic design as a performative practice.
content.plain:
This project was designed in a custom web based tool. For a sneak peak of how this tool looks and behaves, check this recording. (https://are.na/block/24747610). The arcs make shapes based on the proximity of text boxes to one another. Lines exist.
The tool has ephemeral qualities of liveness and performance inscribed into it, leaving the marks of text boxes as they move. Marks slowly disappear over time. This project is an attempt to re frame graphic design as a performative practice.

content.markdown: In collaboration with [Yigit Toprak](https://yigit.world/). We built the an arduino based mechanical prosthetic to create the Duckinator typeface. Each individual controls only one axis, requiring a build up of coordination amongst the duo.
content.html: In collaboration with Yigit Toprak. We built the an arduino based mechanical prosthetic to create the Duckinator typeface. Each individual controls only one axis, requiring a build up of coordination amongst the duo.

content.markdown: Created in my first year at OCAD for Typography 2 class taught by Richard Hunt. The assignment asked to create an event poster that uses material exploration of type to create visual form.

content.markdown: I coded a web experience in collaboration with [Alexandra Maftei](https://al-xndram.github.io/). Alex created a graphic rendering of Cecil Main's poem, _'Blood of a God'_ using stop motion techniques. We worked to assemble these into an interactive narrative for the web.
content.html: I coded a web experience in collaboration with Alexandra Maftei. Alex created a graphic rendering of Cecil Main's poem, 'Blood of a God' using stop motion techniques. We worked to assemble these into an interactive narrative for the web.
content.plain: I coded a web experience in collaboration with Alexandra Maftei (https://al-xndram.github.io/). Alex created a graphic rendering of Cecil Main's poem, 'Blood of a God' using stop motion techniques. We worked to assemble these into an interactive narrative for the web.

content.markdown:
Environmental design in collaboration with [Omama](https://omama.garden/portfolio) for Spice Theory — a  modern Indian fine dining restaurant located in Nagpur, India. 

Visuals were inspired by a balancing of spice-centric illustrations with bold Devanagari typography.

content.markdown:
An Arduino based "printing" device. 

An ultrasound sensor senses how far an object is. Based on the distance draws slits of an image on the canvas, slowly revealing the image with each pull.
content.html:
An Arduino based "printing" device.

An ultrasound sensor senses how far an object is. Based on the distance draws slits of an image on the canvas, slowly revealing the image with each pull.
